team represents [Long Island University](/wiki/Long_Island_University "Long Island University") in [NCAA Division I](/wiki/NCAA_Division_I "NCAA Division I") [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") competition. They play their home games at their Brooklyn Campus in the [Steinberg Wellness Center](/wiki/Steinberg_Wellness_Center "Steinberg Wellness Center") and are members of the [Northeast Conference](/wiki/Northeast_Conference "Northeast Conference"). Their current head coach is Neil Harrow, who was hired in April 2025.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
[*Long Island University Style Guide for Print and Visual Application*](https://s3.amazonaws.com/longislandu/documents/2019/7/25/LIU_Style_Guide.pdf) (PDF). July 25, 2019. Retrieved October 24, 2019.

["LIU combining Post and Brooklyn athletic programs"](https://www.newsday.com/sports/college/liu-post-liu-brooklyn-1.21416636). Newsday. October 3, 2018. Retrieved May 18, 2019.

["#OneLIU website"](https://web.archive.org/web/20190821135943/http://athletics.liu.edu/). Long Island University. Archived from [the original](http://athletics.liu.edu/) on August 21, 2019. Retrieved May 21, 2019.

Basketball team representing Long Island University

The **LIU Sharks women's basketball** team represents [Long Island University](/wiki/Long_Island_University "Long Island University") in [NCAA Division I](/wiki/NCAA_Division_I "NCAA Division I") [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") competition. They play their home games at their Brooklyn Campus in the [Steinberg Wellness Center](/wiki/Steinberg_Wellness_Center "Steinberg Wellness Center") and are members of the [Northeast Conference](/wiki/Northeast_Conference "Northeast Conference"). Their current head coach is Neil Harrow, who was hired in April 2025.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The LIU Sharks are the result of the July 1, 2019 unification of the athletic departments which had previously represented two separate campuses of LIU, the Division I [LIU Brooklyn Blackbirds](/wiki/LIU_Brooklyn_Blackbirds "LIU Brooklyn Blackbirds") and the Division II [LIU Post Pioneers](/wiki/LIU_Post_Pioneers "LIU Post Pioneers").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## NCAA tournament results

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Sharks_women%27s_basketball&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: NCAA tournament results")]

As the Blackbirds, Long Island went to the NCAA Tournament once. Their record is 0-1.

| Year | Seed | Round | Opponent | Result |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [2001](/wiki/2001_NCAA_Division_I_women%27s_basketball_tournament "2001 NCAA Division I women's basketball tournament") | #16 | First Round | #1 Connecticut | L 29-101 |
